
```{r}
hrs16_hcap                 <- readRDS(here::here("R_objects", "A7_075_hrs16_hcap.rds"))
algorithm_thresholds       <- readRDS(here::here("R_objects", "A7_075_algorithm_thresholds.rds"))
v1_algorithm               <- readRDS(here::here("R_objects", "A7_075_v1_algorithm.rds"))
hcap_flow_diagram_values   <- readRDS(here::here("R_objects", "A7_075_hcap_flow_diagram_values.rds"))
```


# Apply algorithm in the HCAP sample



This section descrbes a rule-based classification algorithm applied to the HCAP subsample to assign each respondent to one of three cognitive status categories: **Normal**, **MCI**, or **Dementia**. The algorithm combines a T-scaled cognitive performance score (`TF`), a self-reported memory change item (`PD102`), and an informant-rated cognitive change score (`jorm`). A flow diagram counting function is also defined to document how many respondents pass through each decision node.
Both `algorithm_thresholds` and `v1_algorithm` are saved as function objects, allowing identical classification logic to be applied to future datasets without modifying code.



The HCAP subsample is extracted by filtering to `inHCAP == 1` (N = 3,496).

## Processing Steps

### 1. Threshold Classification (`algorithm_thresholds`)

Two intermediate threshold variables are computed before the final diagnosis is assigned.

#### `cog_threshold` — T-score Cognitive Performance Category

| Condition | `cog_threshold` | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| `TF < 37` | 2 | Substantial impairment |
| `TF < 48` (and ≥ 37) | 1 | Mild impairment |
| `TF ≥ 48` | 0 | Normal range |


#### `jorm_threshold` — Informant-Rated Cognitive Change Category

| Condition | `jorm_threshold` | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| `jorm ≥ 3.4` | 2 | Substantial worsening |
| `3.0 < jorm < 3.4` | 1 | Mild worsening |
| `jorm ≤ 3.0` | 0 | Stable or improved |

The JORM scale runs from 1 (much improved) to 5 (much worse); a score of 3.0 represents "not much change."

#### Self-report memory change

The `PD102` values 8 (refused) and 9 (don't know) are treated equivalently to 3 (worse) in the algorithm — ambiguous self-report responses are classified conservatively toward higher impairment.

The following code shows a function that uses these thresholds to create the data file.

```{r}
#| echo: true
algorithm_thresholds
```

### 2. V1 Classification Algorithm (`v1_algorithm`)

The final three-category diagnosis (`dx_v1`) is assigned using the following decision rules, applied in priority order. A visual of the algorithm is shown in @fig-hrs-algorithm-v1. 

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "HRS algorithm (v1)"
#| label: fig-hrs-algorithm-v1

knitr::include_graphics(here::here("Figures", "HRS-Core-Diagnostic-Algorithm-With-Ns-HRS-Sample-2026-04-16.png"))
```

#### Primary Path — `TF` is Available

| `cog_threshold` | `PD102` | `dx_v1` | Label |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2 (substantial) | 3, 8, or 9 (worse/ambiguous) | 3 | Dementia |
| 2 (substantial) | 1 or 2 (same or better) | 2 | MCI |
| 1 (mild) | 3, 8, or 9 (worse/ambiguous) | 2 | MCI |
| 1 (mild) | 1 or 2 (same or better) | 1 | Normal |
| 0 (normal range) | Any | 1 | Normal |

The logic requires **both** a substantially low cognitive score **and** self-reported worsening to reach a dementia classification. A low score without self-reported worsening, or self-reported worsening with only mild cognitive impairment, results in MCI.

#### Fallback Path — `TF` is Missing

When `TF` is unavailable (e.g., proxy interviews or missing cognitive data), the JORM informant rating is used alone:

| `jorm_threshold` | `dx_v1` | Label |
|---|---|---|
| 2 | 3 | Dementia |
| 1 | 2 | MCI |
| 0 | 1 | Normal |

This is the code that creates a function that implements the algorithm shown in @fig-hrs-algorithm-v1.

```{r}
#| echo: true
v1_algorithm
```

```{r}
#| tbl-cap: "Relationship between algorithmic diagnosis and its constituent components"
#| label: tbl-checkvar-dxv1
QSPtools::checkvar(hrs16_hcap, dx_v1, cog_threshold, PD102, jorm_threshold) %>%
  gt::gt()
```


### 3. Flow Diagram Counts (`v1_flow_diagram`)

A function computes the number of respondents at each decision node in the algorithm, returning a named list with 17 counts:


```{r}

#| tbl-cap: "Sample sizes at various places in the flow diagram"
#| label: tbl-v1-flowdiagram-n

flow_table <- tibble(
  address = c("a1", "b1", "b2", "b3", "b4", "b5",
              "c1", "c2", "c3", "c4",
              "d1", "d2", "d3", "d4",
              "e1", "e2", "e3"),
  Description = c(
    "Total N",
    "Missing TF (routed to JORM fallback)",
    "cog_threshold == 2 (substantial impairment)",
    "cog_threshold in {0, 1} (mild or normal)",
    "cog_threshold == 1 (mild impairment)",
    "cog_threshold == 0 (normal range)",
    "Missing TF AND jorm_threshold == 2",
    "Missing TF AND jorm_threshold in {0, 1}",
    "Missing TF AND jorm_threshold == 1",
    "Missing TF AND jorm_threshold == 0",
    "cog_threshold == 2 AND PD102 worse/ambiguous -> Dementia",
    "cog_threshold == 2 AND PD102 same/better -> MCI",
    "cog_threshold == 1 AND PD102 worse/ambiguous -> MCI",
    "cog_threshold == 1 AND PD102 same/better -> Normal",
    "Final N: Normal (dx_v1 == 1)",
    "Final N: MCI (dx_v1 == 2)",
    "Final N: Dementia (dx_v1 == 3)"
  )
)

flow_table_n <- hcap_flow_diagram_values %>% 
  as_tibble() %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to= "address", values_to = "n") 

flow_table %>%
  left_join(flow_table_n, by = "address") %>%
  gt::gt() 
```



## Descriptive statistics of classifications

```{r}
#| tbl-cap: "Distribution of dementia diagnoses in HCAP"
#| label: tbl-dxv1-distribution
hrs16_hcap %>%
  select(dx_v1, vs1hcapdxeap) %>%
  labelled::to_factor() %>%
  gtsummary::tbl_summary() 

```


```{r}
#| tbl-cap: "Crosstab of version 1 diagnosis and HCAP diagnosis"
#| label: tbl-cross-dxv1-hcap
hrs16_hcap %>%
  labelled::to_factor() %>%
  gtsummary::tbl_cross(vs1hcapdxeap, dx_v1)
```

```{r}
table(hrs16_hcap$vs1hcapdxeap, hrs16_hcap$dx_v1) %>%
  psych::wkappa()
```

```{r}
#| tbl-cap: "Distribution of dementia diagnoses in HCAP (Weighted)"
#| label: tbl-dxv1-distribution-wt
svy_design <- svydesign(
  ids = ~SECU,
  strata = ~STRATUM,
  weights = ~HCAP16WGTR0,
  data = hrs16_hcap,
  nest = TRUE
)

gtsummary::tbl_svysummary(svy_design,
                          include = c("dx_v1", "vs1hcapdxeap"))

```

```{r}
#| tbl-cap: "Crosstab of version 1 diagnosis and HCAP diagnosis (Weighted)"
#| label: tbl-cross-dxv1-hcap-wt

weighted_tab <- xtabs(HCAP16WGTR0 ~ dx_v1 + vs1hcapdxeap, data = hrs16_hcap) 

weighted_tab %>%
  as.data.frame.matrix() %>%
  rownames_to_column("dx_v1") %>%
  gt::gt() %>%
  gt::tab_spanner("HCAP Dementia and MCI Classification", 2:4) %>%
  gt::fmt_number(decimals=0)


```

```{r}
weighted_tab %>%
  psych::wkappa()
```
